textPlain: The title is a bit non descript, so the blog post is exploring> a 15K-array, 2.9M-panel dataset of utility and commercial-grade solar farms across the lower 48 states plus the District of Columbia. This dataset was constructed by a team of researchers including alumni from NOAA, NASA and the USGS.
This will change under the policies of the current U.S. administration.
I am still receiving advertisements from solar companies that want to put panels on farm land. They pay around $3-$4k an acre
